channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts expected to reach far side of moon on Monday - The Guardian
item.description:
1. Artemis II astronauts expected to reach far side of moon on Monday  The Guardian
  2. Artemis II shares new images as it gets closer to the moon  CNN
  3. Artemis II crew hears from NASA Apollo astronaut on Easter  Yahoo
  4. 'Not the moon that I'm used to seeing': Artemis II astronauts describe seeing the far side  NBC News
  5. Artemis II Flight Day 4: Crew Completes Manual Piloting Demonstration  NASA (.gov)

item.title: This is the inspiring Easter message an Artemis 2 astronaut has for Earth while traveling to the moon (video) - Yahoo
item.description:
1. This is the inspiring Easter message an Artemis 2 astronaut has for Earth while traveling to the moon (video)  Yahoo
  2. NASA's Artemis 2 pilot Victor Glover listens to 'Whitey on the Moon' every Monday. This is why.  Space
  3. Latest line: A good week for Victor Glover, a bad week for Sheryl Davis  The Mercury News
  4. Christian astronaut pilots first moon mission in 53 years  The Christian Chronicle
  5. Victor Glover, Artemis II’s Pilot, Will be the First Black Astronaut Around the Moon.  The New York Times

item.title: Students found a star from the dawn of the universe drifting into the Milky Way - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. Students found a star from the dawn of the universe drifting into the Milky Way  ScienceDaily

  3. Traces Of The First Stars May Have Been Discovered  Yahoo
  4. A nearly pristine star from the Large Magellanic Cloud  Nature
  5. The most pristine star yet found in the known universe  Phys.org

item.title: Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13's record - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13's record  Phys.org
  2. More than halfway to the moon, the Artemis II astronauts grappled with a toilet problem  CNN
  3. Artemis II astronauts have toilet trouble on their way towards the Moon  BBC
  4. Moon-bound Artemis II crew reflects on wonder of deep space experience: "You are special in all of this emptiness"  CBS News
  5. Artemis 2 astronauts — now halfway to the moon — report 'burning smell' from toilet, but everything's fine  Yahoo

item.title: The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more' - Space
item.description:
1. The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more'  Space
  2. The Rubin Observatory just turned the night sky into a live feed  Federal News Network
  3. Success Stories: Alerts from the Night Sky  Connected World
  4. Early Data from NSF–DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory Reveals Over 11,000 New Asteroids  Rubin Observatory
  5. Small, Faint, or Fast, Rubin Will Find It  eos.org

item.title: Complex life emerged before the Cambrian explosion - Live Science
item.description:
1. Complex life emerged before the Cambrian explosion  Live Science
  2. Your squishy, wormlike ancestors are at least 4 million years older than you thought  CNN
  3. Newly discovered fossils give scientists first look at the evolution of early complex animals  PBS
  4. Chinese fossils reveal a primordial burst of animal evolution  Reuters
  5. Humans' closest invertebrate ancestors date back much further than thought  Phys.org

item.title: Satellite mirror plans could disrupt sleep and ecosystems worldwide, scientists say - The Guardian
item.description:
1. Satellite mirror plans could disrupt sleep and ecosystems worldwide, scientists say  The Guardian
  2. A million new SpaceX satellites will destroy the night sky—for everyone on Earth  Phys.org
  3. Mirrors in space could disrupt human sleep, scientists warn  Yahoo
  4. Are We About to Permanently Scar the Night Sky With One Million AI Satellite and 50,000 Space Mirrors?  Universe Today
  5. Scientists Warn of Health Risks from Proposed Satellite Mirrors  Oz Arab Media

item.title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby - Space
item.description:
1. Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby  Space
  2. The Artemis II crew has a chance to recreate ‘Earthrise,’ one of the most famous photos of all time  National Geographic
  3. 58 Years After Apollo 8, NASA’s Artemis II Mission May Rediscover Earth  The New York Times
  4. Earthrise 2.0 heralds dawn of the race to settle surface of the moon  observer.co.uk
  5. Looking back on Apollo 8, the first time astronauts went around the moon and back, ahead of Artemis II launch  Houston Public Media

item.title: They Went Snorkeling in a Cave and Found the Floor Covered in Ancient Bones - Indian Defence Review
item.description:
1. They Went Snorkeling in a Cave and Found the Floor Covered in Ancient Bones  Indian Defence Review

  3. Texas researcher uncovers rare prehistoric fossil site in underwater cave  10TV
  4. UT Austin scientists discover Ice Age fossils in underwater cave in Central Texas  KEYE
  5. 'It blew my mind': Long-lost ice-age ecosystem, including fossils of lion-size armadillo and giant ground sloth, discovered in Texas 'water cave'  Yahoo

item.title: Exploding primordial black holes might have reshaped the early universe, and created all matter as we know it - Phys.org
item.description:

  4. Rare Gravitational Wave Discovery May Hold the Key to First Evidence of Primordial Black Holes  The Daily Galaxy

item.title: Scientists Uncover Three Oceans' Worth of Water Stored 700 Kilometers Below Earth's Surface - Indian Defence Review
item.description:
1. Scientists Uncover Three Oceans' Worth of Water Stored 700 Kilometers Below Earth's Surface  Indian Defence Review
  2. A discovery in the Earth's core casts doubt on the origin of much of the planet's water and forces us to rethink what we knew about the formation of the oceans  ECOticias.com
  3. A giant reservoir of water, three times larger than all oceans combined, has been discovered deep beneath the Earth's surface  Українські Національні Новини (УНН)
  4. Scientists discover a hidden ocean 700 kilometres beneath Earth that may be bigger than all oceans  MSN
  5. Scientists have found a massive water reservoir 700 kilometers below, holding more water than Earth’s entire surface  MSN
